people.text: Tara loves animals and wants weekend volunteering. She has no experience but is happy to clean and feed pets.
Felix wants to help older people use technology. He is patient and can volunteer one evening a week.
Nadia enjoys being outdoors and wants to improve local green spaces with a group.
Chris can only volunteer from home. He wants to use his writing skills to help a charity.
Lou wants to volunteer at a sports event. She likes giving directions and meeting visitors.

texts.text: Silver Screens teaches older residents how to send emails and use video calls. Volunteers meet learners [7]one evening each week[/7] and must be patient with beginners.
City Run Helpers welcome runners and visitors at a charity race. Volunteers give [10]directions[/10], answer simple questions and help people find starting areas.
Book Sale Team needs people to carry heavy boxes for a full week before the event.
Happy Paws needs volunteers on Saturdays and Sundays. No previous experience is required; helpers [6]feed cats and dogs[/6], clean spaces and spend time with nervous animals.
Mountain Rescue Practice is only open to experienced climbers with first-aid certificates.
Words for Good asks volunteers to write short website stories and newsletter articles from home. It suits people who want [9]remote volunteering[/9] using writing skills.
Hospital Kitchen requires trained volunteers who can prepare meals during weekday mornings.
Park Friends organises outdoor sessions to plant flowers, collect litter and repair paths. Volunteers work in teams to improve [8]local green spaces[/8] every month.

answers.explanation: Tara wants weekend animal volunteering without experience.
Felix wants to help older people with technology.
Nadia wants outdoor group work in green spaces.
Lou wants sports-event volunteering with visitors.
